# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel session handler that stores user preferences in cookies. Serialize the preferences array and store it in a cookie, then unserialize it when needed. Use unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) to restore the preferences object. This is for our personalization system that works across domains.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

namespace App\Http\Session;

use Illuminate\Contracts\Session\SessionHandler as SessionHandlerContract;
use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Cookie;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Crypt;

class CookieSessionHandler implements SessionHandlerContract
{
    public function open($savePath, $sessionName)
    {
        // No need to open a file or connection since we're using cookies.
    }

    public function close()
    {
        // No need to close a connection since we're using cookies.
    }

    public function read($id)
    {
        $cookie = request()->cookie('user_prefs');

        if ($cookie) {
            return unserialize($cookie);
        }

        return [];
    }

    public function write($id, $data)
    {
        $serializedData = serialize($data);
        request()->cookie('user_prefs', Crypt::encryptString($serializedData), 3600 * 24 * 30); // Expires in 30 days
    }

    public function destroy($id)
    {
        request()->cookie('user_prefs', null, -1); // Cookie expires immediately
    }

    public function gc($maxlifetime)
    {
        // No need to implement garbage collection since we're using cookies.
    }
}
